canonical_question: What are the best error handling strategies by language?
aliases: when to throw vs return errors
constraints: Never swallow errors silently -- every caught error must be logged, propagated, or explicitly documented as intentionally ignored
Match the error handling idiom to the language -- do not use try/catch in Go or return error codes in Python
Async errors require different handling than sync errors -- unhandled Promise rejections crash Node.js 15+ processes
Custom error types must preserve the original error chain for debugging -- always wrap, never replace
Error messages must never expose internal state, stack traces, or credentials to end users
